page: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-2GB is a virtual private server built on the KVM hypervisor, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 1.0 GB per core. Storage is bundled with a 50 GB local SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance alongside moderate multi-core capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves a top-tier score of 3787.27, placing it in the top 10% of tested instances. Single-threaded compression tasks also perform exceptionally well, with gzip compression reaching 40.69 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 17.42 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded performance is more constrained, likely due to the shared CPU allocation and 2-core limit. The multi-threaded bzip3 decompression score of 33.72 GB/sec falls into the bottom 25%, and the Passmark CPU physics test score of 703.56 is similarly positioned in the lower quartile.

Memory performance presents a notable bottleneck for memory-intensive workloads. While cached small-block read bandwidth is measured at 401.49 GB/sec, the Passmark memory score of 950.54 is in the bottom 10%, indicating poor overall memory subsystem performance under broader synthetic workloads. Database operations also show limitations, with Passmark database operations scoring in the bottom 25% at 2404.76. Despite these memory constraints, the server maintains average performance for web serving, delivering 10.31 GB/sec of static web throughput, and handles lightweight LLM inference at 186.2 tokens/sec for text generation. This hardware profile is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and basic development environments.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-2GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM and a 50 GB local SSD. It delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 3787.27 and high single-threaded compression speeds. However, its shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity result in weaker multi-threaded and database performance, with Passmark memory and database scores falling into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that prioritize single-threaded CPU execution over memory density. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and basic development environments.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-2GB is an x86_64 KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 2 GB RAM, and a 50 GB SSD, offering strong single-core performance but constrained memory and multi-threaded capabilities.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-2GB is an x86_64 KVM server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 2 GB RAM, and a 50 GB SSD, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server pairing strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with local SSD storage for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GB RAM
Bundled 50 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Constrained multi-threaded execution and low memory subsystem scores
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded utility tasks
